authority.rule: This file is the single source of truth for current system state.
authority.conflict_resolution: If code, README, plans, or agent reports differ from this file, this file wins until formally updated.

policies.existence_rule: Anything not declared here does not officially exist.
policies.roadmap_rule: Roadmap tracks may be declared here as approved direction, but they do not count as implemented state unless their concrete bridges, systems, migrations, and surfaces are separately marked active or implemented.
policies.topic_area_rule: topic_areas are optional. Apps may reference a topic_area or live directly under a dimension.
policies.app_assignment_rule: Every app must declare a dimension. topic_area is optional, surface_kind is required.

python.note: Canonical runtime bridge via root MBRN-VENV. Supports yfinance, Ollama integration, and Supabase uplink.

local_llm.note: Active local LLM bridge for Week-2 enrichment. Uses local Ollama-backed structured JSON analysis and a separate gold persistence layer.

zeit.note: Standardized v3 naming for the temporal dimension.

20260424_185319_huxiaoman7_skills_eval_m.note: First v5 Bridge dummy deployment from a migrated factory_modules row. Generated as an acceptance surface without requiring live Ollama.

market_sentiment_pipeline.note: Canonical Pillar 3 pipeline. Uses LocalLLMBridge and SupabaseUplink. SecureKeyManager dependencies removed v4.0.

raw_ingest_foundation.note: Implemented generic Week-1 raw ingestion foundation for markets and news. Persists generic raw batches via the raw-ingest Supabase edge function and raw_ingest_* tables.

local_llm_enrichment_worker.note: Implemented Week-2 local enrichment loop. Claims pending raw items, validates structured local LLM output, and persists gold-layer analysis records.

day_zero_autopilot.note: Implemented Week-4 local Windows autopilot. Runs raw collection first, then local LLM enrichment, with Startup-folder shortcut setup and per-run logging. Executes once per Windows login. No Stripe, paywall, premium gating, or frontend commerce changes are part of this system.

mbrn_full_system_autostart.note: Internal Windows Startup launcher invoked by MBRN_Autostart.bat. v5.5 includes the Bridge Agent in autonomous equalizer mode to close the production loop.

gold_oracle_signal_surface.note: Implemented Week-3 secure frontend surface for authenticated reads from the gold_dashboard_items view. Lives under geld -> oracle_signal, not as a full dashboard widget.

sentinel_daemon.note: Internal monitoring and automation daemon. Real and operative, but not part of the public product promise.

mbrn_horizon_scout.note: Experimental R&D discovery scout. Searches GitHub, evaluates local LLM synergy, persists evolution-plan candidates, writes high-ROI integration guides into the Alpha Vault, and obeys the package-free Factory Control pause state.

alpha_vault.note: Experimental one-click integration vault for Scout-discovered high-ROI alphas. Uses frontend, core_logic, and autonomy categories.

admin_entitlement_cli.note: Local-only entitlement tool for internal profile plan_id changes via Supabase service role.

templates_blueprint.note: Blueprint asset exists on disk and is part of the active meta_generator surface.

split_brain_sandbox.note: Split-Brain sandbox controller. Windows host orchestrates isolated CPU-only Linux containers for safe code execution. Ollama/GPU runs natively on host; untrusted code runs in --network none, --memory 512m, --cpus 0.5 Docker cage.

autonomous_dev_agent.note: Self-healing autonomous code generation agent. Uses Ollama/deepseek-coder-v2 to generate Python code, validates it in the mbrn-sandbox, and auto-heals on failure by feeding stderr back to the LLM. Max 5 retries per goal.

mbrn_nexus_bridge.note: Scout-to-Factory closure loop. Reads scout_alphas.json for ROI >= 80 entries by default, can live-read nexus_roi_threshold and pause state from Factory Control, commissions MBRN modules via AutoDevAgent, validates in sandbox, persists factory-ready Python modules, writes dashboard notifications, and can enrich goals with package-free Factory Memory context.

factory_feed_ui.note: Dashboard panel that reads nexus_notifications.json and renders the last 5 autonomously manufactured modules as a live feed. Auto-refreshes every 60s. Integrated into dashboard/index.html via factory-feed-root mount point.

mbrn_factory_memory.note: Package-free JSON/TF-IDF Factory Memory. Stores and retrieves prior alpha snippets for Nexus goal context without ChromaDB or external dependencies. Provides sensor input for Prime Director dry-run control decisions.

mbrn_ouroboros_agent.note: Internal Level-6 autonomy agent for AST-scoped self-mutation. Uses local LLM proposals, syntax checks, runtime safety gate, backup, and rollback for safe code improvement. Prime Director may propose only whitelisted target files; Ouroboros remains the mutation gatekeeper.

mbrn_prime_director.note: Level 5 Meta-Controller. Observes SQLite factory backlog, modules, memory, and logs; checks the factory_paused kill-switch; asks local deepseek-r1:14b for a validated control proposal; actively writes live SQLite control panel decisions.

mbrn_archivist.note: Knowledge Compression Agent. Analyzes factory-ready modules to synthesize master architectural patterns into the factory memory, preventing context bloat for future generation tasks.

mbrn_toolmaker.note: Self-Tooling Agent. Generates and validates standalone Python scripts to resolve Nexus blockages caused by missing logic or complex parsing requirements.

mbrn_sqlite_state_layer.note: v5 Omni state foundation. Provides WAL-mode SQLite tables for scout alphas, notifications, factory control, factory modules, factory memory, seen repos, tool requests, and evolution entries.

mbrn_bridge_agent.note: Factory-to-Frontend bridge. v5.5 includes Autonomous Equalizer mode for self-balancing dimension population and semantic routing.

autonomy_machine.note: Approved execution roadmap for autonomous ingestion, local enrichment, secure frontend rendering, and local autopilot automation. This track defines direction and sequencing, not blanket implemented state.

week_1_data_pipeline.note: Generic raw-data foundation now exists via raw_ingest_foundation, raw-ingest edge ingestion, and raw_ingest_* Supabase tables. Scheduler orchestration remains outside Week 1 scope.

week_2_local_enrichment.note: Canonical local enrichment layer exists via the active local_llm bridge, raw-item processing states, and a separate gold enrichment table.

week_3_secure_frontend.note: Gold data is exposed through the authenticated gold_dashboard_items view and rendered under geld -> oracle_signal. Dashboard remains a compact cockpit entrypoint only.

week_4_day_zero.note: Day Zero is implemented as pure local automation. The Windows autopilot runs the Week-1 collector and then the Week-2 local LLM worker once per Windows login through a Startup-folder shortcut. Stripe, premium gating, paywalls, and frontend commerce remain intentionally untouched.

omni_v5.note: Approved v5 Omni execution track. Roadmap direction is active, but only concrete systems declared in systems count as implemented state.

sprint_1_sqlite_kill_switch.note: SQLite WAL state layer, factory-ready migration, and Prime Director kill-switch are implemented as the v5 foundation.

sprint_2_dimension_queries.note: Horizon Scout uses canonical dimension queries and prioritizes the dimension with the fewest ready/deployed factory modules.

sprint_3_factory_frontend_bridge.note: Bridge agent and hybrid dashboard feed are implemented. Actual generated app count depends on ready module queue and local Ollama availability.

documentation_scope.agent_rule_role: behavioral enforcement for analysis and reporting

archived.note: Files moved to _archive/legacy_python/ during Ghost Cleanup v4.0
archived.reason: Replaced by centralized pipeline_utils.py and LocalLLMBridge architecture
